message:
fix(packs/infrastructure): close arg-as-subcommand bypass in pulumi/terraform safe patterns

Same arg-as-subcommand bypass. terraform and pulumi safe patterns
used `.*?\s+<sub>(?=\s|$)`. A positional arg literally named the same
as the safe subcommand would short-circuit:

  terraform destroy plan              -> matched terraform-plan safe
  terraform destroy --target validate -> matched terraform-validate
  pulumi destroy preview              -> matched pulumi-preview safe
  pulumi stack rm version             -> matched pulumi-version safe

Replaced `.*?` with the flag-walker `(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*` on all
9 pulumi and 11 terraform safe subcommands. Global flags
(`terraform -chdir=./prod plan`, `pulumi --cwd=./prod preview`) still
work; positional args can no longer pose as the subcommand.
